headline: Figure AI (robotics-labs entry) first agent baseline. Functional-band placement supported by: (1) Figure 03 commercial pilot at BMW Spartanburg (production-line deployment); (2) ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participation; (3) CEO Brett Adcock's aggressive 2026 commercial scope including home-robot deployment ambitions (Figure 03 home-use prioritization, washable exterior). NEGATIVE distinguishers vs Agility/Boston Dynamics: Figure AI is NOT a 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory; CEO's aggressive pace prioritizes deployment scale over structured pilot/safety-validation framing; consumer-deployment ambition introduces home-use safety category that pre-dates regulatory framework. Published 48.4 / Functional band sustained. Half-step up on SYS (ASTM safety-standards engagement) offset by half-step down on BND (no anti-weaponization pledge, aggressive consumer-deployment posture without published consent framework). Net composite drift: ~+0.5 points; sub-threshold. Recommend confirm at 48.4 with first-baseline rationale.
key_evidence: Figure 03 commercial pilot at BMW Spartanburg, South Carolina production line — ongoing through 2026.
ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participation alongside Boston Dynamics and Agility (May 27-28 2026 Boston convening).
CEO Brett Adcock 2026 home-robot deployment ambitions: Figure 03 prioritizes home-use, includes washable exterior, suggesting consumer-deployment scope expansion.
Figure AI is NOT a 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory (negative distinguisher vs Boston Dynamics, Agility, ANYbotics, Clearpath, Open Robotics, Unitree).
Aggressive 2026 commercial scope per CEO: production-line deployment + consumer-robot ambitions in same year — pace concern noted by robotics academics.
OpenAI partnership history (terminated 2024); Microsoft Azure infrastructure; $675M Series B at $2.6B valuation context.

evidence.source: vfuturemedia — Humanoid robots enter the workforce: Figure, Boston Dynamics, Tesla Optimus 2026
evidence.finding: Figure 03 BMW Spartanburg pilot ongoing; ASTM safety-standards participation; CEO Adcock home-robot ambitions.

evidence.source: Interesting Engineering — Science fiction become reality in 2026
evidence.finding: Figure 03 home-use prioritization; aggressive 2026 commercial timeline.

evidence.source: The Robot Report — Agility, Boston Dynamics, ASTM to discuss state of humanoid robotics
evidence.finding: Figure AI participation in ASTM safety standards working group with Agility and Boston Dynamics.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 confirmed. A1 detection: BMW pilot context provides operational-issue surfacing; A4 amplification: ASTM working group participation amplifies safety-standards signals.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 confirmed. E1 affective resonance: home-robot ambitions framed around 'help with chores' / human-augmentation rather than empathic-regard architecture explicitly.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 confirmed. AC2 proportionality: BMW pilot operational; AC4 response: incident-response framework not publicly documented but BMW industrial-safety baseline applies.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 confirmed. EQ2 distributional awareness: aggressive consumer-deployment ambition (home-robot) without published equity-impact analysis.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 confirmed. B3 scope clarity: NOT an anti-weaponization pledge signatory (negative); however, no documented weaponization deployment. B5 consent: home-robot deployment introduces consumer-consent framework not yet published.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 confirmed. AB2 transparency: commercial-pilot disclosure partial; AB4 reparation undefined for any humanoid-deployment harm.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 confirmed. S1 causal reasoning: ASTM standards-working-group participation indicates systemic-harm reasoning at industry level (positive). However, aggressive deployment pace vs Agility's structured pilot process is SYS-erosive.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 confirmed. I1 consistency: declared safety commitments; ASTM participation supports consistency; absence of anti-weaponization pledge is INT-erosive sub-threshold.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction confirms 48.4. Functional band sustained. Confirm with first-baseline rationale; sub-threshold drift documented.

negativeSignals: Not an anti-weaponization pledge signatory (vs Boston Dynamics, Agility); aggressive consumer-deployment ambitions outpace regulatory framework; CEO Adcock pace-prioritization concern noted by robotics academics; no published independent third-party safety audit; OpenAI partnership terminated 2024 indicates governance instability.
positiveSignals: BMW Spartanburg commercial pilot operational; ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participation; Figure 03 design includes washable exterior (consumer-safety-positive); structured corporate posture; high-profile investor base provides governance accountability potential.
sector_context: Robotics Labs / commercial-humanoid cluster. Cross-reference: Agility Robotics (anti-weaponization pledge signatory, structured Toyota pilot), Boston Dynamics (anti-weaponization pledge signatory, Hyundai deployment), Apptronik (multi-pilot deployment), 1X Technologies (consumer pre-orders), Tesla Optimus (no pledge, factory deployment). Figure AI distinguished by aggressive 2026 commercial scope without anti-weaponization pledge.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with functional-band confirmation
Anti-weaponization pledge non-signatory status registered as negative distinguisher
open_actions_carried: Watch: ASTM May 27-28 2026 Boston convening outcomes
Watch: Figure 03 home-robot deployment scope and consumer-safety framework publication
Watch: any anti-weaponization pledge signing event
Watch: BMW deployment scaling and worker-impact reporting
Watch: any humanoid-deployment harm or incident report
Note: Figure AI also has separate ai-labs index entry (assessed today separately at composite 37.5)
